description_long: This object is a lifelike representation of a butterfly with striking blue and black wings, accented by white and orange spots along the edges. The wings are broad and slightly translucent, displaying a gradient from deep blue at the tips to lighter blue and white near the body. The body is slender, black, and segmented, with two long, thin antennae extending from the head. The legs are small and mostly hidden beneath the body, while the head is rounded and features a subtle beak-like mouthpart. The overall form is delicate and lightweight, with the wings dominating the visual profile. The coloration and patterning suggest a species adapted for camouflage and display in natural environments.
description: A vivid blue and black butterfly with broad, patterned wings and a slender black body, featuring white and orange spots along the wing edges.
description_view_0: The butterfly is seen from a side angle, highlighting the curvature and gradient of the wings, with the body and antennae clearly visible.
description_view_1: A top-down view reveals the full expanse of both wings, showcasing the symmetrical blue and black pattern with white and orange spots.
description_view_2: The opposite side view emphasizes the lighter blue gradient on the wings and the subtle orange spots near the tips.
description_view_3: A rear angled view displays the underside of the wings and the narrow body, with the antennae extending forward.
attribute_plumage_pattern: Gradient blue with black margins, white and orange spots along wing edges
